name: Table
description: Genuinely functional data table — real column-based sorting (asc/desc/none, toggled by clicking a sortable header) and real row/select-all checkbox selection (reuses Checkbox), rendered as semantic <table>/<thead>/<tbody> markup. TableCellContent provides the leading-visual + title/description cell layout (3 priority levels controlling text emphasis). SortingIcon is the small header sort-direction indicator.
sourceNote: Figma's Table Row Cell defined 6 rigid leading-visual options (checkbox/radio/icon/avatar/brand/company/file-format) — collapsed into one generic `leading` slot on TableCellContent, following the CheckboxCard/DropdownItem/CommandMenuItem pattern. Figma's 'misc' rich-content cell types (Button/ButtonGroup/Toggle/Rating/ProgressBar/StatusBadge/BadgeGroup/AvatarGroup) needed no dedicated wrapper at all — a column's `render` function can return any existing component directly. Rather than reproduce Figma's static demo grid, `Table` is a real functional data table: sortable columns actually re-sort `data` (asc/desc/none cycling), and row/select-all checkboxes maintain real controlled/uncontrolled selection state — following this project's precedent (Pagination, DatePicker, ColorPicker) of favoring functional behavior over literal mockup replication. Figma's header 'Disabled'/'Empty' states were not carried over since a real table's columns don't sensibly disable.

columns.type: { key: string, header: ReactNode, render?: (row, index) => ReactNode, sortable?: boolean, width?: number|string, align?: 'left'|'center'|'right' }[]
